Dense 3D reconstructions are built from the collected depth images using volumetric fusion (TSDF or equivalent).
No TSDF/volumetric fusion for 3D reconstruction mentioned.
━
Pixel-level correspondences between image pairs are generated automatically from the 3D reconstruction (via raycasting/reprojecting), eliminating the need for human annotations.
No auto pixel correspondence generation from 3D reconstruction via raycasting.
━
A fully-convolutional neural network is trained to map RGB images to a dense descriptor space where each pixel receives a D-dimensional descriptor vector.
No FCN for dense per-pixel descriptor mapping mentioned.
━
The network is trained using a pixelwise contrastive loss that minimizes L2 distance for matched pixel descriptors and enforces a margin separation for non-matched descriptors.
No pixelwise contrastive loss for descriptor training mentioned.
━
The non-match loss uses a hinge-like formulation max(0, M-D)^2 where M is a margin parameter and D is the L2 descriptor distance.
Specific hinge formulation max(0,M-D)^2 not mentioned in title/snippet.
━
Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
No hard-negative adaptive scaling by active count mentioned.
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
No 3D change detection for object mask generation mentioned.
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
No mention of restricted match sampling to object pixels via masks.
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
No background domain randomization mentioned.
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
No mention of multiple physically diverse scenes per object for training.
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
No mention of random 180-degree rotation augmentation.
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
No cross-object contrastive loss for multi-object descriptor separation mentioned.
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
No mention of multi-object training on cluttered scenes with 3D geometry.
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
No mention of synthetic multi-object scene generation via mask layering.
━
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
No mention of configurable class-general vs instance-specific descriptor modes.
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
No mention of single forward pass producing dense descriptor map.
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
No L2 nearest-neighbor in descriptor space mentioned.
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
No mention of threshold on descriptor distance for valid match.
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
No mention of computing 3D grasp coordinates from matched pixel + depth + pose.
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
No mention of grasping specific user-designated points across deformed configs.
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
No mention of class-general descriptors for grasp transfer across instances.
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
No mention of instance-specific descriptors with cross-object loss for clutter grasping.
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
Full self-supervised descriptor pipeline without human labels not clearly indicated.
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
Specific architecture (34-layer stride-8 ResNet, bilinear upsampling) not mentioned.
Electronic device and training method of machine learning model
Patent · KR20230065125A
—
… device according to an embodiment may perform representation learning based on self-supervised learning for regularization of a discriminator model. The electronic device may perform visual represen...… device according to an embodiment may perform representation learning based on self-supervised learning for regularization of a discriminator model. The electronic device may perform visual representation training of a machine learning model from an input image even if an image label is not given.

Specific hinge formulation max(0,M-D)^2 not mentioned in title/snippet.
━
Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
No hard-negative adaptive scaling by active count mentioned.
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
No 3D change detection for object mask generation mentioned.
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
No mention of restricted match sampling to object pixels via masks.
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
No background domain randomization mentioned.
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
No mention of multiple physically diverse scenes per object for training.
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
No mention of random 180-degree rotation augmentation.
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
No cross-object contrastive loss for multi-object descriptor separation mentioned.
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
No mention of multi-object training on cluttered scenes with 3D geometry.
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
No mention of synthetic multi-object scene generation via mask layering.
━
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
No mention of configurable class-general vs instance-specific descriptor modes.
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
No mention of single forward pass producing dense descriptor map.
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
No L2 nearest-neighbor in descriptor space mentioned.
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
No mention of threshold on descriptor distance for valid match.
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
No mention of computing 3D grasp coordinates from matched pixel + depth + pose.
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
No mention of grasping specific user-designated points across deformed configs.
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
No mention of class-general descriptors for grasp transfer across instances.
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
No mention of instance-specific descriptors with cross-object loss for clutter grasping.
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
Full self-supervised descriptor pipeline without human labels not clearly indicated.
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
Specific architecture (34-layer stride-8 ResNet, bilinear upsampling) not mentioned.

A method for learning dense visual descriptors uses an RGBD sensor to collect synchronized color and depth data of objects.

Dense 3D reconstructions are built from the collected depth images using volumetric fusion (TSDF or equivalent).
No TSDF/volumetric fusion for 3D reconstruction mentioned.
━

No mention of multi-object training on cluttered scenes with 3D geometry.
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
No mention of synthetic multi-object scene generation via mask layering.
━
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
No mention of configurable class-general vs instance-specific descriptor modes.
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
No mention of single forward pass producing dense descriptor map.
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
No L2 nearest-neighbor in descriptor space mentioned.
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
No mention of threshold on descriptor distance for valid match.
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
No mention of computing 3D grasp coordinates from matched pixel + depth + pose.
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
No mention of grasping specific user-designated points across deformed configs.
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
No mention of class-general descriptors for grasp transfer across instances.
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
No mention of instance-specific descriptors with cross-object loss for clutter grasping.
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
Full self-supervised descriptor pipeline without human labels not clearly indicated.
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
Specific architecture (34-layer stride-8 ResNet, bilinear upsampling) not mentioned.
Objectfusion: Multi-modal 3d object detection with object-centric fusion
Paper
—
… of point cloud representation which is much denser with richer … sign a two-stage fusion scheme for cross-object and cross- … tracking benchmark for 3D multi-object tracking (MOT) task. …… of point cloud representation which is much denser with richer … sign a two-stage fusion scheme for cross-object and cross- … tracking benchmark for 3D multi-object tracking (MOT) task. …
Unmatched Points
━
A method for learning dense visual descriptors uses an RGBD sensor to collect synchronized color and depth data of objects.
No RGBD sensor for object data collection mentioned.
━
Dense 3D reconstructions are built from the collected depth images using volumetric fusion (TSDF or equivalent).
No TSDF/volumetric fusion for 3D reconstruction mentioned.
━
Pixel-level correspondences between image pairs are generated automatically from the 3D reconstruction (via raycasting/reprojecting), eliminating the need for human annotations.
No auto pixel correspondence generation from 3D reconstruction via raycasting.
━
A fully-convolutional neural network is trained to map RGB images to a dense descriptor space where each pixel receives a D-dimensional descriptor vector.
No FCN for dense per-pixel descriptor mapping mentioned.
━
The network is trained using a pixelwise contrastive loss that minimizes L2 distance for matched pixel descriptors and enforces a margin separation for non-matched descriptors.
No pixelwise contrastive loss for descriptor training mentioned.
━
The non-match loss uses a hinge-like formulation max(0, M-D)^2 where M is a margin parameter and D is the L2 descriptor distance.
Specific hinge formulation max(0,M-D)^2 not mentioned in title/snippet.
━
Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
No hard-negative adaptive scaling by active count mentioned.
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
No 3D change detection for object mask generation mentioned.
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
No mention of restricted match sampling to object pixels via masks.
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
No background domain randomization mentioned.
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
No mention of multiple physically diverse scenes per object for training.
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
No mention of random 180-degree rotation augmentation.
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
No cross-object contrastive loss for multi-object descriptor separation mentioned.
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
No mention of multi-object training on cluttered scenes with 3D geometry.
━

No mention of synthetic multi-object scene generation via mask layering.

No mention of configurable class-general vs instance-specific descriptor modes.

In recent years, visual-language contrastive learning has attracted widespread attention because it can obtain a broader feature representation. Learning visual representation from text supervision ha...In recent years, visual-language contrastive learning has attracted widespread attention because it can obtain a broader feature representation. Learning visual representation from text supervision has achieved great success and promoted the emergence of text-supervised semantic segmentation.

━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
No mention of class-general descriptors for grasp transfer across instances.
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
No mention of instance-specific descriptors with cross-object loss for clutter grasping.
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
Full self-supervised descriptor pipeline without human labels not clearly indicated.
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
Specific architecture (34-layer stride-8 ResNet, bilinear upsampling) not mentioned.
Systems and Methods for Contrastive Learning of Visual Representations
Patent · US20210327029A1
—
18 . A computer-implemented method to perform contrastive learning of visual representations, method comprising: obtaining a training image; performing a plurality of first augmentation operations on ...18 . A computer-implemented method to perform contrastive learning of visual representations, method comprising: obtaining a training image; performing a plurality of first augmentation operations on the training image to obtain a first augmented image, wherein the plurality of first augmentation …
Unmatched Points
━
A method for learning dense visual descriptors uses an RGBD sensor to collect synchronized color and depth data of objects.
No RGBD sensor for object data collection mentioned.
━
Dense 3D reconstructions are built from the collected depth images using volumetric fusion (TSDF or equivalent).
No TSDF/volumetric fusion for 3D reconstruction mentioned.
━
Pixel-level correspondences between image pairs are generated automatically from the 3D reconstruction (via raycasting/reprojecting), eliminating the need for human annotations.
No auto pixel correspondence generation from 3D reconstruction via raycasting.
━
A fully-convolutional neural network is trained to map RGB images to a dense descriptor space where each pixel receives a D-dimensional descriptor vector.
No FCN for dense per-pixel descriptor mapping mentioned.
━
The network is trained using a pixelwise contrastive loss that minimizes L2 distance for matched pixel descriptors and enforces a margin separation for non-matched descriptors.
No pixelwise contrastive loss for descriptor training mentioned.
━
The non-match loss uses a hinge-like formulation max(0, M-D)^2 where M is a margin parameter and D is the L2 descriptor distance.
Specific hinge formulation max(0,M-D)^2 not mentioned in title/snippet.
━
Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
No hard-negative adaptive scaling by active count mentioned.
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
No 3D change detection for object mask generation mentioned.
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
No mention of restricted match sampling to object pixels via masks.
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
No background domain randomization mentioned.
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
No mention of multiple physically diverse scenes per object for training.
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
No mention of random 180-degree rotation augmentation.
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
No cross-object contrastive loss for multi-object descriptor separation mentioned.
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
No mention of multi-object training on cluttered scenes with 3D geometry.
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
No mention of synthetic multi-object scene generation via mask layering.
━
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
No mention of configurable class-general vs instance-specific descriptor modes.
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
No mention of single forward pass producing dense descriptor map.
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
No L2 nearest-neighbor in descriptor space mentioned.
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
No mention of threshold on descriptor distance for valid match.
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
No mention of computing 3D grasp coordinates from matched pixel + depth + pose.
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
No mention of grasping specific user-designated points across deformed configs.
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
No mention of class-general descriptors for grasp transfer across instances.
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
No mention of instance-specific descriptors with cross-object loss for clutter grasping.
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
Full self-supervised descriptor pipeline without human labels not clearly indicated.
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
Specific architecture (34-layer stride-8 ResNet, bilinear upsampling) not mentioned.

Mining better samples for contrastive learning of temporal correspondence
Paper
—
… from the observation in metric learning literature [53, 46] that using too hard or too easy negative samples may produce worse representations, we collect semihard negative samples by …… from the observation in metric learning literature [53, 46] that using too hard or too easy negative samples may produce worse representations, we collect semihard negative samples by …
Unmatched Points
━
A method for learning dense visual descriptors uses an RGBD sensor to collect synchronized color and depth data of objects.
No clear mention of RGBD sensor for collecting color and depth data for dense descriptor learning
━
Dense 3D reconstructions are built from the collected depth images using volumetric fusion (TSDF or equivalent).
No mention of dense 3D reconstruction via volumetric fusion from depth images
━
Pixel-level correspondences between image pairs are generated automatically from the 3D reconstruction (via raycasting/reprojecting), eliminating the need for human annotations.
No mention of automatically generating pixel correspondences from 3D reconstruction via raycasting
━
A fully-convolutional neural network is trained to map RGB images to a dense descriptor space where each pixel receives a D-dimensional descriptor vector.
No mention of training a fully-convolutional network for dense per-pixel descriptors
━
The network is trained using a pixelwise contrastive loss that minimizes L2 distance for matched pixel descriptors and enforces a margin separation for non-matched descriptors.
No mention of pixelwise contrastive loss for descriptor training with match/non-match formulation
━
The non-match loss uses a hinge-like formulation max(0, M-D)^2 where M is a margin parameter and D is the L2 descriptor distance.
No mention of hinge-like non-match loss formulation with margin parameter
━
Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
No mention of hard-negative adaptive scaling for non-match loss normalization
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
No mention of isolating object geometry via 3D change detection to produce automatic masks
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
No mention of restricting match sampling to object pixels using automatic masks
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
No mention of background domain randomization to force descriptor invariance to scene context
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
No mention of multiple physically diverse scenes per object for training
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
No mention of random 180-degree rotation augmentation
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
No mention of cross-object contrastive loss for forcing distinct descriptor regions
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
No mention of direct multi-object training on physical cluttered scenes using 3D geometry
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
No mention of synthetic multi-object scenes via mask layering with occlusion pruning
━
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
No mention of configurable class-general vs instance-specific descriptor training
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
No mention of single forward pass producing dense descriptor map at inference
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
No mention of L2 nearest-neighbor search in descriptor space for point correspondence
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
No mention of threshold on descriptor distance determining match validity
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
No mention of using depth and camera pose for 3D target coordinates for robotic grasping
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
No mention of grasping specific user-designated points across deformed configurations
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
No mention of class-general descriptors enabling grasp transfer across object instances
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
No mention of instance-specific descriptors with cross-object loss for grasping in clutter
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
No mention of entirely self-supervised pipeline from data collection to trained model
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
No mention of specific FCN architecture (34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling

A fully-convolutional neural network is trained to map RGB images to a dense descriptor space where each pixel receives a D-dimensional descriptor vector.
No mention of training a fully-convolutional network for dense per-pixel descriptors
━
The network is trained using a pixelwise contrastive loss that minimizes L2 distance for matched pixel descriptors and enforces a margin separation for non-matched descriptors.
No mention of pixelwise contrastive loss for descriptor training with match/non-match formulation
━
The non-match loss uses a hinge-like formulation max(0, M-D)^2 where M is a margin parameter and D is the L2 descriptor distance.
No mention of hinge-like non-match loss formulation with margin parameter
━
Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
No mention of hard-negative adaptive scaling for non-match loss normalization
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
No mention of isolating object geometry via 3D change detection to produce automatic masks
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
No mention of restricting match sampling to object pixels using automatic masks
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
No mention of background domain randomization to force descriptor invariance to scene context
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
No mention of multiple physically diverse scenes per object for training
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
No mention of random 180-degree rotation augmentation
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
No mention of cross-object contrastive loss for forcing distinct descriptor regions
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
No mention of direct multi-object training on physical cluttered scenes using 3D geometry
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
No mention of synthetic multi-object scenes via mask layering with occlusion pruning
━
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
No mention of configurable class-general vs instance-specific descriptor training
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
No mention of single forward pass producing dense descriptor map at inference
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
No mention of L2 nearest-neighbor search in descriptor space for point correspondence
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
No mention of threshold on descriptor distance determining match validity
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
No mention of using depth and camera pose for 3D target coordinates for robotic grasping
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
No mention of grasping specific user-designated points across deformed configurations
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
No mention of class-general descriptors enabling grasp transfer across object instances
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
No mention of instance-specific descriptors with cross-object loss for grasping in clutter
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
No mention of entirely self-supervised pipeline from data collection to trained model
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
No mention of specific FCN architecture (34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling

The non-match loss uses a hinge-like formulation max(0, M-D)^2 where M is a margin parameter and D is the L2 descriptor distance.
No mention of hinge-like non-match loss formulation with margin parameter
━
Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
No mention of hard-negative adaptive scaling for non-match loss normalization
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
No mention of isolating object geometry via 3D change detection to produce automatic masks
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
No mention of restricting match sampling to object pixels using automatic masks
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
No mention of background domain randomization to force descriptor invariance to scene context
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
No mention of multiple physically diverse scenes per object for training
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
No mention of random 180-degree rotation augmentation
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
No mention of cross-object contrastive loss for forcing distinct descriptor regions
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
No mention of direct multi-object training on physical cluttered scenes using 3D geometry
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
No mention of synthetic multi-object scenes via mask layering with occlusion pruning

The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
No mention of configurable class-general vs instance-specific descriptor training
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
No mention of single forward pass producing dense descriptor map at inference
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
No mention of L2 nearest-neighbor search in descriptor space for point correspondence
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
No mention of threshold on descriptor distance determining match validity
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.

No mention of hinge-like non-match loss formulation with margin parameter
━
Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
No mention of hard-negative adaptive scaling for non-match loss normalization
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
No mention of isolating object geometry via 3D change detection to produce automatic masks
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
No mention of restricting match sampling to object pixels using automatic masks
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
Mentions domain randomization with varied backgrounds for robotic object recognition, but not specifically about replacing backgrounds using automatic object masks for dense descriptor invariance
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
No mention of multiple physically diverse scenes per object for training
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
No mention of random 180-degree rotation augmentation
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
No mention of cross-object contrastive loss for forcing distinct descriptor regions
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
No mention of direct multi-object training on physical cluttered scenes using 3D geometry
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
No mention of synthetic multi-object scenes via mask layering with occlusion pruning
━
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
No mention of configurable class-general vs instance-specific descriptor training
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
No mention of single forward pass producing dense descriptor map at inference
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
No mention of L2 nearest-neighbor search in descriptor space for point correspondence
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
No mention of threshold on descriptor distance determining match validity
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
No mention of using depth and camera pose for 3D target coordinates for robotic grasping
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
No mention of grasping specific user-designated points across deformed configurations
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
No mention of class-general descriptors enabling grasp transfer across object instances
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
No mention of instance-specific descriptors with cross-object loss for grasping in clutter
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
No mention of entirely self-supervised pipeline from data collection to trained model
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
No mention of specific FCN architecture (34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling

━
Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
No mention of hard-negative adaptive scaling for non-match loss normalization
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
No mention of isolating object geometry via 3D change detection to produce automatic masks
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
No mention of restricting match sampling to object pixels using automatic masks
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
Uses domain randomization with non-realistic textures for sim-to-real grasping transfer, not specifically about replacing backgrounds using automatic object masks for dense descriptor invariance
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
No mention of multiple physically diverse scenes per object for training
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
No mention of random 180-degree rotation augmentation
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
No mention of cross-object contrastive loss for forcing distinct descriptor regions
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
No mention of direct multi-object training on physical cluttered scenes using 3D geometry
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
No mention of synthetic multi-object scenes via mask layering with occlusion pruning
━
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
No mention of configurable class-general vs instance-specific descriptor training
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
No mention of single forward pass producing dense descriptor map at inference
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
No mention of L2 nearest-neighbor search in descriptor space for point correspondence
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
No mention of threshold on descriptor distance determining match validity
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
No mention of using depth and camera pose for 3D target coordinates for robotic grasping
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
No mention of grasping specific user-designated points across deformed configurations
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
No mention of class-general descriptors enabling grasp transfer across object instances
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
No mention of instance-specific descriptors with cross-object loss for grasping in clutter
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
No mention of entirely self-supervised pipeline from data collection to trained model
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
No mention of specific FCN architecture (34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling
Surfemb: Dense and continuous correspondence distributions for object pose estimation with learnt surface embeddings
Paper
—
… a contrastive loss, sampling from the surface during training. … We use 12 embedding dimensions and have a separate key … Dense object nets: Learning dense visual object descriptors …… a contrastive loss, sampling from the surface during training. … We use 12 embedding dimensions and have a separate key … Dense object nets: Learning dense visual object descriptors …
Unmatched Points
━
A method for learning dense visual descriptors uses an RGBD sensor to collect synchronized color and depth data of objects.
No clear mention of RGBD sensor for collecting color and depth data for dense descriptor learning
━
Dense 3D reconstructions are built from the collected depth images using volumetric fusion (TSDF or equivalent).
No mention of dense 3D reconstruction via volumetric fusion from depth images
━
Pixel-level correspondences between image pairs are generated automatically from the 3D reconstruction (via raycasting/reprojecting), eliminating the need for human annotations.
No mention of automatically generating pixel correspondences from 3D reconstruction via raycasting
━
A fully-convolutional neural network is trained to map RGB images to a dense descriptor space where each pixel receives a D-dimensional descriptor vector.
The 'Dense Object Nets' reference in the snippet is a citation of prior work, not the paper's own method. Paper proposes surface embeddings for pose estimation, not a FCN for dense per-pixel descriptor maps
━
The network is trained using a pixelwise contrastive loss that minimizes L2 distance for matched pixel descriptors and enforces a margin separation for non-matched descriptors.
Uses contrastive loss for learning surface embeddings for pose estimation, not pixelwise contrastive loss between image pairs for dense descriptor training as in the invention

The non-match loss uses a hinge-like formulation max(0, M-D)^2 where M is a margin parameter and D is the L2 descriptor distance.

… more robust because it handles the point cloud directly in a collection rather than relying on exact matching of individual points. Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
No mention of hard-negative adaptive scaling for non-match loss normalization
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
No mention of isolating object geometry via 3D change detection to produce automatic masks
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
No mention of restricting match sampling to object pixels using automatic masks
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
No mention of background domain randomization to force descriptor invariance to scene context
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
No mention of multiple physically diverse scenes per object for training
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
No mention of random 180-degree rotation augmentation
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
No mention of cross-object contrastive loss for forcing distinct descriptor regions
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
No mention of direct multi-object training on physical cluttered scenes using 3D geometry
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
No mention of synthetic multi-object scenes via mask layering with occlusion pruning
━
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
No mention of configurable class-general vs instance-specific descriptor training
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
No mention of single forward pass producing dense descriptor map at inference
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
No mention of L2 nearest-neighbor search in descriptor space for point correspondence
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
Mentions distance threshold for defining positive pairs during descriptor training, but C18 is about thresholding descriptor distance at inference to determine match validity
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
No mention of using depth and camera pose for 3D target coordinates for robotic grasping
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
No mention of grasping specific user-designated points across deformed configurations
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
No mention of class-general descriptors enabling grasp transfer across object instances
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
No mention of instance-specific descriptors with cross-object loss for grasping in clutter
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
No mention of entirely self-supervised pipeline from data collection to trained model
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
No mention of specific FCN architecture (34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling

Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
No mention of hard-negative adaptive scaling for non-match loss normalization
━
Object geometry is isolated via 3D change detection (comparing reconstructions with and without the object) to produce automatic per-image binary object masks.
No mention of isolating object geometry via 3D change detection to produce automatic masks
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
No mention of restricting match sampling to object pixels using automatic masks
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
No mention of background domain randomization to force descriptor invariance to scene context
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
No mention of multiple physically diverse scenes per object for training
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
No mention of random 180-degree rotation augmentation
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
No mention of cross-object contrastive loss for forcing distinct descriptor regions
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
No mention of direct multi-object training on physical cluttered scenes using 3D geometry
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
No mention of synthetic multi-object scenes via mask layering with occlusion pruning
━
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
No mention of configurable class-general vs instance-specific descriptor training
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
No mention of single forward pass producing dense descriptor map at inference
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
No mention of L2 nearest-neighbor search in descriptor space for point correspondence
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
No mention of threshold on descriptor distance determining match validity
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
No mention of using depth and camera pose for 3D target coordinates for robotic grasping
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
No mention of grasping specific user-designated points across deformed configurations
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
No mention of class-general descriptors enabling grasp transfer across object instances

Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
No mention of instance-specific descriptors with cross-object loss for grasping in clutter

The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.

No mention of isolating object geometry via 3D change detection to produce automatic masks
━
Match sampling is restricted to object pixels using the automatic masks, while non-matches can sample from the full image including background.
No mention of restricting match sampling to object pixels using automatic masks
━
Background domain randomization replaces background regions with random images using the automatic object masks to force descriptors to be invariant to scene context.
No mention of background domain randomization to force descriptor invariance to scene context
━
Training data includes multiple physically diverse scenes per object with varied configurations, orientations, and lighting conditions.
No mention of multiple physically diverse scenes per object for training
━
Synthetic image augmentation includes random 180-degree rotations applied to training images.
No mention of random 180-degree rotation augmentation
━
For multi-object settings, a cross-object contrastive loss treats all pixel pairs between different objects as non-matches to force distinct regions in descriptor space.
No mention of cross-object contrastive loss for forcing distinct descriptor regions
━
Multi-object training can use direct training on physical cluttered scenes where 3D geometry provides data associations without individual object labels.
No mention of direct multi-object training on physical cluttered scenes using 3D geometry
━
Synthetic multi-object scenes are generated by computationally layering single-object masks, with correspondences that become occluded during layering pruned from the training signal.
No mention of synthetic multi-object scenes via mask layering with occlusion pruning
━
The training procedure can be configured to produce either class-general descriptors (that generalize across instances of a class) or instance-specific descriptors (that distinguish individual instances within a class).
No mention of configurable class-general vs instance-specific descriptor training
━
At inference, a single forward pass through the trained network produces a dense descriptor map for a new RGB image without additional processing.
No mention of single forward pass producing dense descriptor map at inference
━
Corresponding points between a reference image and a new scene are found via L2 nearest-neighbor search in the dense descriptor space.
No mention of L2 nearest-neighbor search in descriptor space for point correspondence
━
A threshold on descriptor distance determines whether a valid match exists in the new scene.
No mention of threshold on descriptor distance determining match validity
━
The system uses the matched pixel location with depth data and camera pose to determine 3D coordinates of the target point for robotic grasping.
No mention of using depth and camera pose for 3D target coordinates for robotic grasping
━
The method enables grasping of specific user-designated points on objects across potentially deformed configurations.
No mention of grasping specific user-designated points across deformed configurations
━
Class-general descriptors enable transferring specific grasps across different instances of the same object class.
No mention of class-general descriptors enabling grasp transfer across object instances
━
Instance-specific descriptors combined with cross-object loss enable grasping target points on specific objects in cluttered multi-object scenes.
No mention of instance-specific descriptors with cross-object loss for grasping in clutter
━
The entire pipeline from data collection to trained descriptor model uses only self-supervision with no human-provided labels or annotations.
No mention of entirely self-supervised pipeline from data collection to trained model
━
The network architecture is a fully-convolutional network (e.g., 34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling to produce full-resolution descriptor maps.
No mention of specific FCN architecture (34-layer stride-8 ResNet) with bilinear upsampling

Unmatched Points
━
A method for learning dense visual descriptors uses an RGBD sensor to collect synchronized color and depth data of objects.
No clear mention of RGBD sensor for collecting color and depth data for dense descriptor learning
━
Dense 3D reconstructions are built from the collected depth images using volumetric fusion (TSDF or equivalent).
No mention of dense 3D reconstruction via volumetric fusion from depth images
━
Pixel-level correspondences between image pairs are generated automatically from the 3D reconstruction (via raycasting/reprojecting), eliminating the need for human annotations.
No mention of automatically generating pixel correspondences from 3D reconstruction via raycasting
━
A fully-convolutional neural network is trained to map RGB images to a dense descriptor space where each pixel receives a D-dimensional descriptor vector.
No mention of training a fully-convolutional network for dense per-pixel descriptors
━
The network is trained using a pixelwise contrastive loss that minimizes L2 distance for matched pixel descriptors and enforces a margin separation for non-matched descriptors.
No mention of pixelwise contrastive loss for descriptor training with match/non-match formulation
━
The non-match loss uses a hinge-like formulation max(0, M-D)^2 where M is a margin parameter and D is the L2 descriptor distance.
No mention of hinge-like non-match loss formulation with margin parameter
━
Hard-negative adaptive scaling normalizes the non-match loss component by the count of active hard negatives rather than total non-match count.
No mention of hard-negative adaptive scaling for non-match loss normalization
